sections.paragraph:
Ἠσαῦ, ὁ (Ἡσαῦ; עֵשָׂו) indecl. (LXX; TestJob 1:6; Test12Patr; Philo; Just.; in Joseph. Ἠσαῦς, αῦ [Ant. 2, 5]) Esau Hb 11:20. ‘Dis-favored’ by God Ro 9:13 (Mal 1:2f). Typical of an immoral and vulgar person (on Esau in this light cp. Book of Jubilees 35, 13f; Philo, Virt. 208; s. Gen 27:41; 28:7f) Hb 12:16. The flight of Jacob fr. Esau (Gen 27:41ff) 1 Cl 4:8.—TW.

indecl. indecl. = indeclinable

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

Test12Patr Test12Patr = Testaments of the Twelve Patriarchs, with interpolations II b.c.–III a.d.—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Jubilees Jubilees = Book of J.—List 5

  1. fr. = from

ff ff = (and) following

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 440
